home.heroDescription: Generate ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript, and more config files with stack-based presets. No more copy-pasting from docs.
home.popularPresetsDescription: Pick a preset to start with pre-configured options

steps.title: Choose a file or stack
steps.description: Pick a config file or start with a preset stack

steps.title: Customize options
steps.description: Toggle settings with checkboxes, dropdowns, and toggles

steps.title: Copy or Download
steps.description: Get individual files or download everything as a ZIP

home.supportedFilesDescription: Generate 9 essential config files for your project with customizable options.

faqs.question: Are the generated files up to date?
faqs.answer: Yes. We follow the latest official documentation and best practices for each tool.

faqs.question: Can I use these in production?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The generated configs are production-ready and follow community standards.

faqs.question: Do I need to download all files?
faqs.answer: No. You can copy individual files or download only what you need.

faqs.question: Is my data sent to a server?
faqs.answer: No. Everything runs in your browser. No data is sent anywhere.

generator.title: Config Generator
generator.description: Generate production-ready config files for your project. Start with a stack preset or pick individual files.
generator.stackPresetsDescription: Pre-configured bundles for popular stacks. Pick one to generate all config files at once.
generator.individualFilesDescription: Generate a single config file with full option control and live preview.
generator.methodStackTitle: Generate all at once with a stack
generator.methodStackDescription: Pick a project stack to generate every required config file in one go. Best for quickly starting a new project.
generator.methodFileDescription: Use this when you want to add or replace specific files in an already configured project.

migration.title: Migrate to latest format
migration.dropzone: Drop your config file here or paste code below

footer.tagline: Build project config files with stack-based presets.

migration.hubDescription: Paste a legacy config and modernize it, or audit your current setup against best practices. Everything runs in your browser — your code never leaves your machine.

eslint.name: ESLint
eslint.description: Convert .eslintrc (JSON / CommonJS) to ESLint v9 flat config and audit deprecated rules.

prettier.name: Prettier
prettier.description: Detect deprecated options and review your Prettier config against current recommendations.

tsconfig.name: TSConfig
tsconfig.description: Find deprecated TypeScript compiler options and missing recommended settings.

page.eslintDescription: Paste your .eslintrc (JSON or CommonJS) and migrate to ESLint v9 flat config. Deprecated rules are detected and explained.
page.prettierDescription: Audit your Prettier config for deprecated options and review against current recommendations.
page.tsconfigDescription: Audit tsconfig.json for deprecated compiler options and missing recommended settings.
page.emptyPreview: Your migrated or normalized config will appear here once you paste an input.

supportedFormats.tsconfig: Supported: tsconfig.json (with comments and trailing commas)

tsconfigDisclaimer.topBody: This tool diagnoses deprecated and missing recommended options in tsconfig.json. The recommended values are not the only correct answers — the optimal settings depend on your project type (monorepo / library / app) and build target (ESNext / Node / Browser). Use the audit results as a reference and decide in your own project context.
tsconfigDisclaimer.resultBody: The diagnostics below are based on recommended values. Appropriate settings can vary depending on whether your project is a monorepo, a library, or an app.

mismatchWarning.title: This looks like a different tool's config
mismatchWarning.description: Your input looks like a {detectedTool} config, but this page is for {currentTool}. Go to the right tool page for accurate results.
mismatchWarning.blockedNotice: Conversion blocked due to tool type mismatch.

seo.homeTitle: ConfigDeck — Build your project config in seconds
seo.homeDescription: Generate ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript, and more config files with stack-based presets. No more copy-pasting from docs.
seo.generatorDescription: Generate production-ready config files for your project. Start with a stack preset or pick individual files.
seo.migrationHubTitle: Config Migration & Audit — ESLint, Prettier, TSConfig | ConfigDeck
seo.migrationHubDescription: Migrate your .eslintrc to flat config, audit Prettier options, and diagnose tsconfig.json. Paste your config file and get instant analysis in your browser — no uploads, no accounts.
seo.migrationEslintTitle: ESLint Legacy to Flat Config Migration | ConfigDeck
seo.migrationEslintDescription: Convert .eslintrc (JSON, CommonJS) to eslint.config.mjs flat config. Detect deprecated rules, check eslint-config-prettier conflicts, and get recommended rule suggestions — all in your browser.
seo.migrationPrettierTitle: Prettier Config Audit — Deprecated Options & Recommendations | ConfigDeck
seo.migrationPrettierDescription: Audit your .prettierrc or prettier.config.js for deprecated options and Prettier 3.x compatibility. Get actionable recommendations without sending your config anywhere.
seo.migrationTsconfigTitle: tsconfig.json Audit — Strict Options & Recommended Values | ConfigDeck
seo.migrationTsconfigDescription: Audit your tsconfig.json for missing strict options, deprecated settings, and recommended compilerOptions. Results are advisory — best values depend on your project type (monorepo, library, or app).
seo.docsDescription: Learn how to use ConfigDeck to generate config files for your project. Guides, API reference, and more.
seo.blogDescription: Updates, tutorials, and insights about developer tooling, config management, and ConfigDeck features.
seo.articleDescription: Stay up to date with the latest updates and releases from ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript, and more.

docs.title: Documentation

page.title: AI Tool Config + Skills Generator
page.heading: AI Tool Config + Skills Generator
page.description: Pick a stack and generate AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, Cursor MDC, Copilot instructions, and popular Agent Skills in one click.

step1.title: Select Tools
step1.legend: Select all the AI coding tools you use
step1.minSelectError: Please select at least one tool.

step3.title: Best Practices
step3.intro: Expand a category to pick items. All items are listed regardless of stack.
step3.additionalNotesPlaceholder: Add team conventions or extra instructions. English input recommended (better AI accuracy).

step4.title: Boundaries
step4.intro: Define guardrails for the AI in three tiers. Optional — you can also add custom items.

output.emptyTitle: Select a stack and tools to see generated files here

modeOnly.title: ⓘ Claude Code only mode
modeOnly.descriptionBefore: Since you use only Claude Code, CLAUDE.md is the single source of truth. The AGENTS.md import (

modeMixed.title: ⓘ Claude Code with other tools
modeMixed.descriptionBefore: Claude Code does not read AGENTS.md directly, so we auto-insert
modeMixed.descriptionAfter: into CLAUDE.md. One AGENTS.md keeps all four tools in sync.

modeStandalone.title: ⓘ Claude Code selected
modeStandalone.descriptionBefore: If you also use other AI tools, leave the "Claude Code only" toggle off and select those tools as well.

hiddenFilesNotice.title: 📂 Watch out for hidden files
hiddenFilesNotice.description: Folders that start with a dot (`.cursor/`, `.claude/`, `.github/`) are hidden by default in macOS Finder.
